questions.q: P. C. Mahalanobis is regarded as the founder of which scientific discipline in India?
questions.explanation: Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis is widely regarded as the father of modern statistics in India.

questions.q: Which famous research institute did P. C. Mahalanobis found?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis founded the Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), which became a world centre for statistics.

questions.q: A widely used statistical measure of distance is named after Mahalanobis. What does it measure?
options: How far a point lies from a distribution
The speed of a chemical reaction
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance measures how many standard deviations away a point is from the mean of a distribution, accounting for correlations.

questions.q: In which Indian city is the Indian Statistical Institute, founded by Mahalanobis, headquartered?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute is headquartered in Kolkata (then Calcutta), where Mahalanobis established it.

questions.q: Mahalanobis played a major role in shaping which aspect of independent India?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was a key architect of India's economic planning, especially the Second Five-Year Plan.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation 'ISI', the institute Mahalanobis founded, stand for?
questions.explanation: ISI stands for the Indian Statistical Institute, founded by Mahalanobis in 1931.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance is a concept primarily used in which field today?
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance is widely used in statistics, data science and machine learning for tasks like outlier detection and classification.

questions.q: P. C. Mahalanobis was originally trained as a scientist in which subject before turning to statistics?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis studied physics at Cambridge and worked in physics before devoting himself to statistics.

questions.q: India observes National Statistics Day on 29 June each year to honour which scientist?
questions.explanation: National Statistics Day is celebrated on 29 June, the birth anniversary of P. C. Mahalanobis.

questions.q: Which technique pioneered by Mahalanobis is used to estimate quantities like crop yields by surveying a small fraction of an area?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis pioneered the use of large-scale sample surveys, applying them to estimate crop acreage and yields across India.

questions.q: In which year did Mahalanobis formally found the Indian Statistical Institute?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute was founded in 1931, growing out of the Statistical Laboratory at Presidency College, Calcutta.

questions.q: At which English university did Mahalanobis study, an experience that introduced him to statistics?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis studied at King's College, Cambridge, where he encountered the journal Biometrika that sparked his interest in statistics.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance differs from ordinary Euclidean distance chiefly because it accounts for what?
options: The colour of the data points
The correlations and variances among the variables
questions.explanation: Unlike Euclidean distance, the Mahalanobis distance incorporates the covariance structure, scaling and correlations of the variables.

questions.q: The economic strategy associated with Mahalanobis in India's Second Five-Year Plan emphasized which priority?
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis model underpinning the Second Five-Year Plan (1956) emphasized rapid development of heavy industry and capital goods.

questions.q: Mahalanobis originally developed his distance measure while analysing data from which field?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis introduced his distance while studying anthropometric measurements to compare and classify population groups.

questions.q: Mahalanobis founded a major statistics journal in 1933. What is it called?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis founded Sankhya, The Indian Journal of Statistics, in 1933; 'sankhya' is a Sanskrit word for number.

questions.q: Which national survey organisation, created to collect socio-economic data across India, owes much to Mahalanobis's methods?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was instrumental in establishing the National Sample Survey (NSS) in 1950 to gather socio-economic data.

questions.q: Mahalanobis was a member of which body that drafted India's Five-Year Plans?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis served as a member of the Planning Commission of India and was its leading statistical adviser.

questions.q: In what year was P. C. Mahalanobis born, an anniversary now marked as Statistics Day?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was born on 29 June 1893 in Calcutta; his birthday is observed as National Statistics Day.

questions.q: Which of India's highest civilian honours was awarded to Mahalanobis in 1968?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours, in 1968.

questions.q: Mahalanobis introduced an idea for estimating sampling error by repeatedly using sub-samples. What is this technique often called?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis devised 'interpenetrating samples' (interpenetrating network of subsamples) to estimate and control survey errors.

questions.q: Which renowned statistician worked closely with Mahalanobis at the Indian Statistical Institute early in his career?
questions.explanation: C. R. Rao was a leading statistician mentored at and associated with the Indian Statistical Institute under Mahalanobis.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance for a point x from a distribution with mean μ uses which matrix in its formula?
options: The inverse of the covariance matrix
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance is defined using the inverse of the covariance matrix: D = sqrt((x-μ)ᵀ S⁻¹ (x-μ)).

questions.q: In which year did Mahalanobis first publish his statistical distance measure?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis published his formulation of the distance, 'On the generalized distance in statistics', in 1936.

questions.q: Mahalanobis was elected a Fellow of which prestigious British scientific body in 1945?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) of London in 1945 for his contributions to statistics.

questions.q: The two-sector growth model Mahalanobis built for India's Second Plan is mathematically related to which earlier economist's work?
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis model closely parallels the earlier Soviet growth model of G. A. Feldman, emphasizing investment in capital goods.

questions.q: Before turning fully to statistics, Mahalanobis briefly taught and conducted research in physics at which Calcutta college?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis taught physics at Presidency College, Calcutta, where he also set up a statistical laboratory that grew into the ISI.

questions.q: When all variables are uncorrelated and have unit variance, the Mahalanobis distance reduces to which familiar distance?
questions.explanation: If the covariance matrix is the identity matrix, the Mahalanobis distance simplifies exactly to the ordinary Euclidean distance.

questions.q: The Indian Statistical Institute was recognised by a special Act of Parliament in 1959 that allowed it to do what?
questions.explanation: The Indian Statistical Institute Act of 1959 declared it an institution of national importance and empowered it to grant degrees.

questions.q: Mahalanobis applied statistical sampling to a famous practical problem early on. What was it?
options: Estimating jute and rice crop yields in Bengal
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis applied large-scale sample surveys to estimate crop acreage and yields, including jute and rice in Bengal.
